manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: impetuousness, endearment, heinousness, randomization, damnation, value, suspicion, willpower, integration.
Your opponent's team words are: mercy, rhetoric, viewpoint, flimflam, peculiarity, hilarity, shrewdness, afterworld.
Distractor words are: instigation, amuck, stigma, desirability, avoidance, takings, despondency.
The kill words are: proneness.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue and list of target words (from our team) that clue relates to. Must avoid giving clue that is part of any words (no same substring). Provide only clue and targets.

We need to consider whether any of those target words could be guessed as opponent or distractor. But we just need to give clue and targets; they will guess later.

We must ensure clue is not one of the words in any list or contains parts of them. Single-word clue; cannot contain parts of them. "Part of them" probably means cannot include substrings like "will", "value". Safer to pick something abstract linking multiple.

Our target words: impetuousness, endearment, heinousness, randomization, damnation, value, suspicion, willpower, integration.

We could try clue "psychology" linking suspicion, willpower, integration maybe value? But "psychology" could be okay.

But need to list which ones clue relates to. Could be many words. But we must be careful not to be too ambiguous leading to opponent picks.

Maybe choose clue "morality": could relate to damnation, heinousness, value, suspicion? But also could link to opponent "mercy". That could be risky. But we just give clue and list of target words we intend. The partner will know which targets to guess; they might guess opponent illegally; but we don't control that.

Goal: maximize number of correct guesses maybe pick a clue that covers many. Could choose "self" linking willpower, integration, suspicion? Not sure.

Maybe pick multiple: "emotion" linking endearment, suspicion? Not.

"Chance" links randomization, maybe impetuousness? hmm.

"Integrity" links willpower, integration, value, maybe suspicion? Might be okay.

Check substrings: "integrity" contains "integr" which is part of integration? The rule: clue cannot contain parts of them. "integr" is part of "integration". That might be disallowed. Safer to avoid.

"Fortitude": links willpower, impetuousness? maybe.

"Destiny": hmm.

Maybe give separate clues sequentially? The prompt wants one clue now; we can list multiple targets. So choose something like "drive" linking willpower, impetuousness, integration? Not sure.

Better choose clue "risk": could relate to impetuousness (risk-taking), randomization (risk), suspicion? Not great.

Maybe "sin": links damnation, heinousness. Could also relate to opponent "mercy"? Possibly.

But we want to include multiple: maybe choose clue "morbid". No.

Let's think of a clue that clearly points to a subset of our words but not opponent words.

We can select 3-4 words to guess.

Potential grouping: "psychic" linking suspicion, willpower, integration? Not.

"Decision" linking willpower, integration, value, suspicion? Hmm.

"Choice" similar.

Maybe pick "principle": links value, integration, willpower? Possibly.

But also "principle" could be related to opponent "viewpoint"? Not dangerous.

Let's pick clue "principle". It contains "prin" etc not part of any words. Good.

Target words: value, willpower, integration, suspicion? Not sure if "principle" directly relates to suspicion. Probably not. Could just list three: value, willpower, integration.

Thus clue "principle". Provide list.

Check rule: clue cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them. "principle" shares "prin" not a word. It's fine.

Now output format:

CLUE: principle
TARGETS: value, willpower, integration

Make sure no extra text
